3: import (
4: 	"strings"
5: 
6: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/types/action"
7: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/types/resource"
8: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/sdk/pbs/controller/api/resources/scopes"
9: )
10: 
11: const (
12: 	AnyAuthenticatedUserId = "u_auth"
13: 	AnonymousUserId        = "u_anon"
14: 	RecoveryUserId         = "u_recovery"

17: // ACL provides an entry point into the permissions engine for determining if an
18: // action is allowed on a resource based on a principal's (user or group) grants.
19: type ACL struct {
20: 	scopeMap map[string][]Grant
21: }
22: 
23: // ACLResults provides a type for the permission's engine results so that we can
24: // pass more detailed information along in the future if we want. It was useful
25: // in Vault, may be useful here.
26: type ACLResults struct {
27: 	AuthenticationFinished bool
28: 	Authorized             bool
29: 	OutputFields           OutputFieldsMap
30: 
31: 	// This is included but unexported for testing/debugging
32: 	scopeMap map[string][]Grant
33: }
34: 
35: // Permission provides information about the specific
36: // resources that a user has been granted access to for a given scope, resource, and action.
37: type Permission struct {
38: 	ScopeId  string // The scope id for which the permission applies.
39: 	Resource resource.Type
40: 	Action   action.Type
41: 
42: 	ResourceIds []string // Any specific resource ids that have been referred in the grant's `id` field, if applicable.
43: 	OnlySelf    bool     // The grant only allows actions against the user's own resources.
44: 	All         bool     // We got a wildcard in the grant string's `id` field.
45: }
46: 
47: // UserPermissions is a set of Permissions for a User.
48: type UserPermissions struct {
49: 	UserId      string
50: 	Permissions []Permission
51: }
52: 
53: // Resource defines something within boundary that requires authorization
54: // capabilities. Resources must have a ScopeId.
55: type Resource struct {
56: 	// ScopeId is the scope that contains the Resource.
57: 	ScopeId string `json:"scope_id,omitempty"`
58: 
59: 	// Id is the public id of the resource.
60: 	Id string `json:"id,omitempty"`
61: 
62: 	// Type of resource.
63: 	Type resource.Type `json:"type,omitempty"`
64: 
65: 	// Pin if defined would constrain the resource within the collection of the
66: 	// pin id.
67: 	Pin string `json:"pin,omitempty"`
68: }
69: 
70: // NewACL creates an ACL from the grants provided.
71: func NewACL(grants ...Grant) ACL {
72: 	ret := ACL{
73: 		scopeMap: make(map[string][]Grant, len(grants)),
74: 	}
75: 
76: 	for _, grant := range grants {
77: 		ret.scopeMap[grant.scope.Id] = append(ret.scopeMap[grant.scope.Id], grant)
78: 	}
79: 
80: 	return ret
81: }
82: 
83: // Allowed determines if the grants for an ACL allow an action for a resource.
84: func (a ACL) Allowed(r Resource, aType action.Type, userId string, opt ...Option) (results ACLResults) {
85: 	opts := getOpts(opt...)
86: 
87: 	// First, get the grants within the specified scope
88: 	grants := a.scopeMap[r.ScopeId]
89: 	results.scopeMap = a.scopeMap
90: 
91: 	var parentAction action.Type
92: 	split := strings.Split(aType.String(), ":")
93: 	if len(split) == 2 {
94: 		parentAction = action.Map[split[0]]
95: 	}
96: 	// Now, go through and check the cases indicated above
97: 	for _, grant := range grants {
98: 		var outputFieldsOnly bool
99: 		switch {
100: 		case len(grant.actions) == 0:
101: 			// Continue with the next grant, unless we have output fields
102: 			// specified in which case we continue to be able to apply the
103: 			// output fields depending on ID and type.
104: 			if len(grant.OutputFields) > 0 {
105: 				outputFieldsOnly = true
106: 			} else {
107: 				continue
108: 			}
109: 		case grant.actions[aType]:
110: 			// We have this action
111: 		case grant.actions[parentAction]:
112: 			// We don't have this action, but it's a subaction and we have the
113: 			// parent action. As an example, if we are looking for "read:self"
114: 			// and have "read", this is sufficient.
115: 		case grant.actions[action.All]:
116: 			// All actions are allowed
117: 		default:
118: 			// No actions in the grant match what we're looking for, so continue
119: 			// with the next grant
120: 			continue
121: 		}
122: 
123: 		// We step through all grants, to fetch the full list of output fields.
124: 		// However, we shortcut if we find *.
125: 		//
126: 		// If the action was not found above but we did find output fields in
127: 		// patterns that match, we do not authorize the request, but we do build
128: 		// up the output fields patterns.
129: 		//
130: 		// Note that when using IsActionOrParent it is merely to test whether it
131: 		// is an allowed format since some formats operate ony on collections
132: 		// (or don't operate at all on collections) and we want to ensure that
133: 		// it is/isn't a create or list command or subcommand to know whether
134: 		// that form is valid. The actual checking of whether the given action
135: 		// is granted to the user already happened above.
136: 		var found bool
137: 		switch {
138: 		// Case 1: We only allow specific actions on specific types for the
139: 		// anonymous user. ID being supplied or not doesn't matter in this case,
140: 		// it must be an explicit type and action(s); adding this as an explicit
141: 		// case here prevents duplicating logic in two of the other more
142: 		// general-purpose cases below (3 and 4). See notes there about ID being
143: 		// present or not.
144: 		case !opts.withSkipAnonymousUserRestrictions &&
145: 			(userId == AnonymousUserId || userId == ""):
146: 			switch {
147: 			// Allow discovery of scopes, so that auth methods within can be
148: 			// discovered
149: 			case grant.typ == r.Type &&
150: 				grant.typ == resource.Scope &&
151: 				(aType == action.List || aType == action.NoOp):
152: 				found = true
153: 
154: 			// Allow discovery of and authenticating to auth methods
155: 			case grant.typ == r.Type &&
156: 				grant.typ == resource.AuthMethod &&
157: 				(aType == action.List || aType == action.NoOp || aType == action.Authenticate):
158: 				found = true
159: 			}
160: 
161: 		// Case 2:
162: 		// id=<resource.id>;actions=<action> where ID cannot be a wildcard; or
163: 		// id=<resource.id>;output_fields=<fields> where fields cannot be a
164: 		// wildcard.
165: 		case grant.id == r.Id &&
166: 			grant.id != "" &&
167: 			grant.id != "*" &&
168: 			grant.typ == resource.Unknown &&
169: 			!action.List.IsActionOrParent(aType) &&
170: 			!action.Create.IsActionOrParent(aType):
171: 
172: 			found = true
173: 
174: 		// Case 3: type=<resource.type>;actions=<action> when action is list or
175: 		// create. Must be a top level collection, otherwise must be one of the
176: 		// two formats specified in cases 4 or 5. Or,
177: 		// type=resource.type;output_fields=<fields> and no action. This is more
178: 		// of a semantic difference compared to 4 more than a security
179: 		// difference; this type is for clarity as it ties more closely to the
180: 		// concept of create and list as actions on a collection, operating on a
181: 		// collection directly. The format in case 4 will still work for
182: 		// create/list on collections but that's more of a shortcut to allow
183: 		// things like id=*;type=*;actions=* for admin flows so that you don't
184: 		// need to separate out explicit collection actions into separate typed
185: 		// grants for each collection within a role. This does mean there are
186: 		// "two ways of doing things" but it's a reasonable UX tradeoff given
187: 		// that "all IDs" can reasonably be construed to include "and the one
188: 		// I'm making" and "all of them for listing".
189: 		case grant.id == "" &&
190: 			r.Id == "" &&
191: 			grant.typ == r.Type &&
192: 			grant.typ != resource.Unknown &&
193: 			topLevelType(r.Type) &&
194: 			(action.List.IsActionOrParent(aType) ||
195: 				action.Create.IsActionOrParent(aType)):
196: 
197: 			found = true
198: 
199: 		// Case 4:
200: 		// id=*;type=<resource.type>;actions=<action> where type cannot be
201: 		// unknown but can be a wildcard to allow any resource at all; or
202: 		// id=*;type=<resource.type>;output_fields=<fields> with no action.
203: 		case grant.id == "*" &&
204: 			grant.typ != resource.Unknown &&
205: 			(grant.typ == r.Type ||
206: 				grant.typ == resource.All):
207: 
208: 			found = true
209: 
210: 		// Case 5:
211: 		// id=<pin>;type=<resource.type>;actions=<action> where type can be a
212: 		// wildcard and this this is operating on a non-top-level type. Same for
213: 		// output fields only.
214: 		case grant.id != "" &&
215: 			grant.id == r.Pin &&
216: 			grant.typ != resource.Unknown &&
217: 			(grant.typ == r.Type || grant.typ == resource.All) &&
218: 			!topLevelType(r.Type):
219: 
220: 			found = true
221: 		}
222: 
223: 		if found {
224: 			if !outputFieldsOnly {
225: 				results.Authorized = true
226: 			}
227: 			if results.OutputFields = results.OutputFields.AddFields(grant.OutputFields.Fields()); results.OutputFields.HasAll() && results.Authorized {
228: 				return
229: 			}
230: 		}
231: 	}
232: 	return
233: }
234: 
235: // ListPermissions builds a set of Permissions based on the grants in the ACL.
236: // Permissions are determined for the given resource for each of the provided scopes.
237: // There must be a grant for a given resource for one of the provided "id actions"
238: // or for action.All in order for a Permission to be created for the scope.
239: // The set of "id actions" is resource dependant, but will generally include all
240: // actions that can be taken on an individual resource.
241: func (a ACL) ListPermissions(requestedScopes map[string]*scopes.ScopeInfo,
242: 	requestedType resource.Type,
243: 	idActions action.ActionSet,
244: 	userId string,
245: ) []Permission {
246: 	perms := make([]Permission, 0, len(requestedScopes))
247: 	for scopeId := range requestedScopes {
248: 		p := Permission{
249: 			ScopeId:  scopeId,
250: 			Resource: requestedType,
251: 			Action:   action.List,
252: 			OnlySelf: true, // default to only self to be restrictive
253: 		}
254: 		if userId == RecoveryUserId {
255: 			p.All = true
256: 			p.OnlySelf = false
257: 			perms = append(perms, p)
258: 			continue
259: 		}
260: 
261: 		// Get grants for a specific scope id from the source of truth.
262: 		grants := a.scopeMap[scopeId]
263: 		for _, grant := range grants {
264: 			// This grant doesn't match what we're looking for, ignore.
265: 			if grant.typ != requestedType && grant.typ != resource.All {
266: 				continue
267: 			}
268: 
269: 			// We found a grant that matches the requested resource type:
270: 			// Search to see if one or all actions in the action set have been granted.
271: 			found := false
272: 			if ok := grant.actions[action.All]; ok {
273: 				found = true
274: 			} else {
275: 				for _, a := range idActions {
276: 					if ok := grant.actions[a]; ok {
277: 						found = true
278: 						break
279: 					}
280: 				}
281: 			}
282: 			if !found { // In this case, none of the requested actions were granted for the given scope id.
283: 				continue
284: 			}
285: 
286: 			actions, _ := grant.Actions()
287: 			excludeList := make(action.ActionSet, 0, len(actions))
288: 			for _, aa := range actions {
289: 				if aa != action.List {
290: 					excludeList = append(excludeList, aa)
291: 				}
292: 			}
293: 			p.OnlySelf = p.OnlySelf && excludeList.OnlySelf()
294: 
295: 			switch grant.id {
296: 			case "*":
297: 				p.All = true
298: 			case "":
299: 				continue
300: 			default:
301: 				p.ResourceIds = append(p.ResourceIds, grant.id)
302: 			}
303: 		}
304: 
305: 		if p.All || len(p.ResourceIds) > 0 {
306: 			perms = append(perms, p)
307: 		}
308: 	}
309: 
310: 	return perms
311: }
312: 
313: func topLevelType(typ resource.Type) bool {
314: 	switch typ {
315: 	case resource.AuthMethod,
316: 		resource.AuthToken,
317: 		resource.CredentialStore,
318: 		resource.Group,
319: 		resource.HostCatalog,
320: 		resource.Role,
321: 		resource.Scope,
322: 		resource.Session,
323: 		resource.Target,
324: 		resource.User,
325: 		resource.Worker:
326: 		return true
327: 	}
328: 	return false
329: }

18: )

20: // CreateScope will create a scope in the repository and return the written
21: // scope. Supported options include: WithPublicId and WithRandomReader.
22: func (r *Repository) CreateScope(ctx context.Context, s *Scope, userId string, opt ...Option) (*Scope, error) {
23: 	const op = "iam.(Repository).CreateScope"
24: 	if s == nil {
25: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing scope")
26: 	}
27: 	if s.Scope == nil {
28: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing scope store")
29: 	}
30: 	if s.PublicId != "" {
31: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "public id not empty")
32: 	}
33: 
34: 	var parentOplogWrapper wrapping.Wrapper
35: 	var err error
36: 	switch s.Type {
37: 	case scope.Unknown.String():
38: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "unknown type")
39: 	case scope.Global.String():
40: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "invalid type")
41: 	default:
42: 		switch s.ParentId {
43: 		case "":
44: 			return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing parent id")
45: 		case scope.Global.String():
46: 			parentOplogWrapper, err = r.kms.GetWrapper(ctx, scope.Global.String(), kms.KeyPurposeOplog)
47: 		default:
48: 			parentOplogWrapper, err = r.kms.GetWrapper(ctx, s.ParentId, kms.KeyPurposeOplog)
49: 		}
50: 	}
51: 	if err != nil {
52: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "unable to get oplog wrapper")
53: 	}
54: 
55: 	opts := getOpts(opt...)
56: 
57: 	var scopePublicId string
58: 	var scopeMetadata oplog.Metadata
59: 	var scopeRaw any
60: 	{
61: 		scopeType := scope.Map[s.Type]
62: 		if opts.withPublicId != "" {
63: 			if !strings.HasPrefix(opts.withPublicId, scopeType.Prefix()+"_") {
64: 				return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, fmt.Sprintf("passed-in public ID %q has wrong prefix for type %q which uses prefix %q", opts.withPublicId, scopeType.String(), scopeType.Prefix()))
65: 			}
66: 			scopePublicId = opts.withPublicId
67: 		} else {
68: 			scopePublicId, err = newScopeId(scopeType)
69: 			if err != nil {
70: 				return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
71: 			}
72: 		}
73: 		sc := s.Clone().(*Scope)
74: 		sc.PublicId = scopePublicId
75: 		scopeRaw = sc
76: 		scopeMetadata, err = r.stdMetadata(ctx, sc)
77: 		if err != nil {
78: 			return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
79: 		}
80: 		scopeMetadata["op-type"] = []string{oplog.OpType_OP_TYPE_CREATE.String()}
81: 	}
82: 
83: 	var adminRolePublicId string
84: 	var adminRoleMetadata oplog.Metadata
85: 	var adminRole *Role
86: 	var adminRoleRaw any
87: 	switch {
88: 	case userId == "",
89: 		userId == perms.AnonymousUserId,
90: 		userId == perms.AnyAuthenticatedUserId,
91: 		userId == perms.RecoveryUserId,
92: 		opts.withSkipAdminRoleCreation:
93: 		// TODO: Cause a log entry. The repo doesn't have a logger right now,
94: 		// and ideally we will be using context to pass around log info scoped
95: 		// to this request for grouped display in the server log. The only
96: 		// reason this should ever happen anyways is via the administrative
97: 		// recovery workflow so it's already a special case.
98: 
99: 		// Also, stop linter from complaining
100: 		_ = adminRole
101: 
102: 	default:
103: 		adminRole, err = NewRole(scopePublicId)
104: 		if err != nil {
105: 			return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("error instantiating new admin role"))
106: 		}
107: 		adminRolePublicId, err = newRoleId()
108: 		if err != nil {
109: 			return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("error generating public id for new admin role"))
110: 		}
111: 		adminRole.PublicId = adminRolePublicId
112: 		adminRole.Name = "Administration"
113: 		adminRole.Description = fmt.Sprintf("Role created for administration of scope %s by user %s at its creation time", scopePublicId, userId)
114: 		adminRoleRaw = adminRole
115: 		adminRoleMetadata = oplog.Metadata{
116: 			"resource-public-id": []string{adminRolePublicId},
117: 			"scope-id":           []string{scopePublicId},
118: 			"scope-type":         []string{s.Type},
119: 			"resource-type":      []string{resource.Role.String()},
120: 			"op-type":            []string{oplog.OpType_OP_TYPE_CREATE.String()},
121: 		}
122: 	}
123: 
124: 	var defaultRolePublicId string
125: 	var defaultRoleMetadata oplog.Metadata
126: 	var defaultRole *Role
127: 	var defaultRoleRaw any
128: 	if !opts.withSkipDefaultRoleCreation {
129: 		defaultRole, err = NewRole(scopePublicId)
130: 		if err != nil {
131: 			return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("error instantiating new default role"))
132: 		}
133: 		defaultRolePublicId, err = newRoleId()
134: 		if err != nil {
135: 			return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("error generating public id for new default role"))
136: 		}
137: 		defaultRole.PublicId = defaultRolePublicId
138: 		switch s.Type {
139: 		case scope.Project.String():
140: 			defaultRole.Name = "Default Grants"
141: 			defaultRole.Description = fmt.Sprintf("Role created to provide default grants to users of scope %s at its creation time", scopePublicId)
142: 		default:
143: 			defaultRole.Name = "Login and Default Grants"
144: 			defaultRole.Description = fmt.Sprintf("Role created for login capability, account self-management, and other default grants for users of scope %s at its creation time", scopePublicId)
145: 		}
146: 		defaultRoleRaw = defaultRole
147: 		defaultRoleMetadata = oplog.Metadata{
148: 			"resource-public-id": []string{defaultRolePublicId},
149: 			"scope-id":           []string{scopePublicId},
150: 			"scope-type":         []string{s.Type},
151: 			"resource-type":      []string{resource.Role.String()},
152: 			"op-type":            []string{oplog.OpType_OP_TYPE_CREATE.String()},
153: 		}
154: 	}
155: 
156: 	reader := opts.withRandomReader
157: 	if reader == nil {
158: 		reader = rand.Reader
159: 	}
160: 
161: 	_, err = r.writer.DoTx(
162: 		ctx,
163: 		db.StdRetryCnt,
164: 		db.ExpBackoff{},
165: 		func(dbr db.Reader, w db.Writer) error {
166: 			if err := w.Create(
167: 				ctx,
168: 				scopeRaw,
169: 				db.WithOplog(parentOplogWrapper, scopeMetadata),
170: 			); err != nil {
171: 				return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("error creating scope"))
172: 			}
173: 
174: 			s := scopeRaw.(*Scope)
175: 
176: 			txnKms, err := kms.NewUsingReaderWriter(ctx, dbr, w)
177: 			if err != nil {
178: 				return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("error creating transaction's kms"))

380: // UpdateScope will update a scope in the repository and return the written
381: // scope.  fieldMaskPaths provides field_mask.proto paths for fields that should
382: // be updated.  Fields will be set to NULL if the field is a zero value and
383: // included in fieldMask. Name and Description are the only updatable fields,
384: // and everything else is ignored.  If no updatable fields are included in the
385: // fieldMaskPaths, then an error is returned.
386: func (r *Repository) UpdateScope(ctx context.Context, scope *Scope, version uint32, fieldMaskPaths []string, _ ...Option) (*Scope, int, error) {
387: 	const op = "iam.(Repository).UpdateScope"
388: 	if scope == nil {
389: 		return nil, db.NoRowsAffected, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing scope")
390: 	}
391: 	if scope.PublicId == "" {
392: 		return nil, db.NoRowsAffected, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing public id")
393: 	}
394: 	if contains(fieldMaskPaths, "ParentId") {
395: 		return nil, db.NoRowsAffected, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidFieldMask, op, "you cannot change a scope's parent")
396: 	}
397: 	var dbMask, nullFields []string
398: 	dbMask, nullFields = dbw.BuildUpdatePaths(
399: 		map[string]any{
400: 			"name":                scope.Name,
401: 			"description":         scope.Description,
402: 			"PrimaryAuthMethodId": scope.PrimaryAuthMethodId, // gorm: it's important that the field start with a capital letter.
403: 		},
404: 		fieldMaskPaths,
405: 		nil,
406: 	)
407: 	// nada to update, so reload scope from db and return it
408: 	if len(dbMask) == 0 && len(nullFields) == 0 {
409: 		return nil, db.NoRowsAffected, errors.E(ctx, errors.WithCode(errors.EmptyFieldMask), errors.WithOp(op))
410: 	}
411: 	resource, rowsUpdated, err := r.update(ctx, scope, version, dbMask, nullFields)
412: 	if err != nil {
413: 		if errors.IsUniqueError(err) {
414: 			return nil, db.NoRowsAffected, errors.New(ctx, errors.NotUnique, op, fmt.Sprintf("%s name %s already exists", scope.PublicId, scope.Name))
415: 		}
416: 		return nil, db.NoRowsAffected, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg(fmt.Sprintf("for public id %s", scope.PublicId)))
417: 	}
418: 	return resource.(*Scope), rowsUpdated, nil

421: // LookupScope will look up a scope in the repository.  If the scope is not
422: // found, it will return nil, nil.
423: func (r *Repository) LookupScope(ctx context.Context, withPublicId string, _ ...Option) (*Scope, error) {
424: 	const op = "iam.(Repository).LookupScope"

426: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing public id")
427: 	}

429: 	scope.PublicId = withPublicId

431: 		if errors.IsNotFoundError(err) {
432: 			return nil, nil
433: 		}
434: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg(fmt.Sprintf("failed for %s", withPublicId)))
435: 	}
436: 	return &scope, nil
437: }

439: // DeleteScope will delete a scope from the repository
440: func (r *Repository) DeleteScope(ctx context.Context, withPublicId string, _ ...Option) (int, error) {
441: 	const op = "iam.(Repository).DeleteScope"
442: 	if withPublicId == "" {
443: 		return db.NoRowsAffected, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing public id")
444: 	}
445: 	if withPublicId == scope.Global.String() {
446: 		return db.NoRowsAffected, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "invalid to delete global scope")
447: 	}
448: 	scope := AllocScope()
449: 	scope.PublicId = withPublicId
450: 	rowsDeleted, err := r.delete(ctx, &scope)
451: 	if err != nil {
452: 		if errors.Is(err, ErrMetadataScopeNotFound) {
453: 			return 0, nil
454: 		}
455: 		return db.NoRowsAffected, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg(fmt.Sprintf("failed for %s", withPublicId)))
456: 	}
457: 	return rowsDeleted, nil
458: }

460: // ListScopes with the parent IDs, supports the WithLimit option.
461: func (r *Repository) ListScopes(ctx context.Context, withParentIds []string, opt ...Option) ([]*Scope, error) {
462: 	const op = "iam.(Repository).ListScopes"
463: 	if len(withParentIds) == 0 {
464: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing parent id")
465: 	}
466: 	var items []*Scope
467: 	err := r.list(ctx, &items, "parent_id in (?)", []any{withParentIds}, opt...)
468: 	if err != nil {
469: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
470: 	}
471: 	return items, nil
472: }

474: // ListScopesRecursively allows for recursive listing of scopes based on a root scope
475: // ID. It returns the root scope ID as a part of the set.
476: func (r *Repository) ListScopesRecursively(ctx context.Context, rootScopeId string, opt ...Option) ([]*Scope, error) {
477: 	const op = "iam.(Repository).ListRecursively"
478: 	var scopes []*Scope
479: 	var where string
480: 	var args []any
481: 	switch {
482: 	case rootScopeId == scope.Global.String():
483: 		// Nothing -- we want all scopes
484: 	case strings.HasPrefix(rootScopeId, "o_"):
485: 		// The org itself and any projects that have it as parent
486: 		where = "public_id = ? or parent_id = ?"
487: 		args = append(args, rootScopeId, rootScopeId)
488: 	case strings.HasPrefix(rootScopeId, "p_"):
489: 		// No scopes can (currently) live under projects, so just the project
490: 		// itself
491: 		where = "public_id = ?"
492: 		args = append(args, rootScopeId)
493: 	default:
494: 		// We have no idea what scope type this is so bail
495: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidPublicId, op+":TypeSwitch", "invalid scope ID")
496: 	}
497: 	err := r.list(ctx, &scopes, where, args, opt...)
498: 	if err != nil {
499: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op+":ListQuery")
500: 	}
501: 	return scopes, nil
502: }
